title: DG Matrix Closes $60 Million Series A to Scale Next-Generation Power Infrastructure for AI and Electrification
authors_or_org: DG Matrix, Inc. (press release via Business Wire)
claim_supported: DG Matrix (Morrisville, NC) closed a $60M Series A (Feb 18 2026, led by Engine Ventures, with ABB, Mitsubishi Heavy Industries participating) to scale its Interport multi-port solid-state transformer platform for AI datacenters and electrification -- a direct, venture-funded, ABB-backed competitor to the MVDC hybrid SSCB thesis, already shipping commercial pilots.
locator: Press release body: funding amount, lead investor, investor list, product name.
notes: Confirms the MV power-electronics-for-AI-datacenter niche is already attracting well-funded, ABB-backed specialized entrants ahead of the idea's 2028-2030 plan; a material red-team finding not in the idea's competition_outlook (which named only Hitachi/Siemens/GE).

india_origin_audit.notes: DG Matrix HQ Morrisville, North Carolina, USA.

title: DG Matrix Selects Infineon as a Key Silicon Carbide Supplier for Its Multi-Port Solid-State Transformer Platform
authors_or_org: DG Matrix, Inc.; Infineon Technologies AG
claim_supported: DG Matrix will source latest-generation SiC MOSFETs from Infineon for its Interport and Interport Cell-LV solid-state transformer systems 'deployed in AI data centers and electrification applications worldwide,' confirming the competitor already has product in deployment, not just a lab prototype.
locator: Press release body: supplier relationship and deployment-status language.
notes: Corroborates S01; independent confirmation that DG Matrix product is past pilot stage.

india_origin_audit.notes: Infineon HQ Neubiberg, Germany; DG Matrix HQ USA (see S01).

title: Amperesand Raises $80M Series A Co-led by Walden Catalyst Ventures and Temasek to Redefine Power Infrastructure for AI Data Centers
authors_or_org: Amperesand Pte. Ltd. (press release via Business Wire; profile via Latitude Media)
claim_supported: Amperesand -- incubated by Xora Innovation and spun out of Singapore's Nanyang Technological University in 2023, with operations in Singapore, San Francisco, and Reno -- raised an $80M Series A (Nov 2025) targeting 30MW of commercial MV solid-state-transformer systems to hyperscale AI-datacenter customers in 2026, ahead of the idea's 2028-2030 plan.
locator: Article body: origin/incubation sentence, funding amount, investor list, 2026 30MW deployment target.
notes: GEOGRAPHY CAVEAT: Amperesand is Singapore-originated (NTU spinout, Singapore operations retained). Per binding project rule, Singapore is excluded from buyer examples, market sizing, and competitor/entry-route scoring. This record is retained ONLY as risk-disclosure context (a well-funded rival with US manufacturing hubs exists) and MUST NOT be used to justify Singapore as a market or to size US/China opportunity. Do not treat as a US-headquartered competitor.

institutions.name: Amperesand Pte. Ltd. / Xora Innovation / NTU Singapore

india_origin_audit.notes: Singapore origin (NTU spinout); not India. India-origin exclusion rule does not apply, but separate binding Singapore-market-exclusion rule does -- see notes field above.

title: Inside the 800VDC Revolution, Part 1 (OCP Diablo 400 specification, deployment phasing, TAM)
claim_supported: OCP's Diablo 400 spec (co-authored by Google, Meta, Microsoft; draft v0.5.2 released May 2025) targets 100kW-1MW per IT rack; SemiAnalysis projects Phase 3 (late 2028/2029) facility-level 800VDC distribution becomes viable with centralized rectifiers, Phase 4 (>2029) solid-state-transformer deployment begins, ~39GW of incremental 800VDC-powered capacity by 2030, and SST TAM ~$32B by 2030.
locator: Article body: OCP Diablo 400 authorship/release-date paragraph; phased deployment-window table; 39GW/2030 and $32B SST TAM figures.
notes: Independent (non-government) second 2030-window timing source, corroborating the primary/official Southern Spirit HVDC (atlas L08-041, DOE-backed, construction 2029/in-service 2032) and ARPA-E DC-GRIDS (atlas L02-034) triggers already in the atlas. OCP's own primary spec pages (opencompute.org) returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch this session; this analyst-firm secondary synthesis is used instead and labeled as such.

india_origin_audit.notes: SemiAnalysis is a US-based semiconductor/technology research and analysis newsletter.

title: PRC-028-1 & PRC-029-1: Implementation Plans & Data Submittals (NSRF presentation, March 26, 2026)
authors_or_org: Texas Reliability Entity (Texas RE), presenter Blake Ianni
claim_supported: Texas RE (the NERC regional entity covering ERCOT) describes PRC-029-1 compliance evidence as EMT/dynamic-model submission validated against actual disturbance-event data collected via PRC-028-1 monitoring equipment -- model- and passive-monitoring-based verification, with no requirement identified for active field MW-hardware disturbance-injection testing at plant sites.
locator: 23-page NSRF presentation PDF, compliance-evidence-framework slides (model submission + monitoring-data validation).
notes: DIRECTLY ANSWERS the P3R2-A-03 FIX gate ('obtain written regional-entity compliance guidance... evidencing that PRC-029 verification will require/pay for field MW hardware rather than EMT-model submissions'). This written regional-entity guidance points the OPPOSITE direction from what the gate needed to pass: model + passive-monitoring compliance, not field MW hardware. WebFetch returned a summarized reading of the PDF (binary fetch, not raw text extraction) -- verifier should re-extract full text before further reliance, but the summarized compliance-framework finding is consistent across two independently fetched sources (this and S02/S03).

india_origin_audit.notes: Texas RE HQ Austin, Texas, USA; NERC regional entity.

title: What You Need to Know About PRC-028-1: Inverter-Based Resource Data Collection
authors_or_org: HSI (Health & Safety Institute / HSI Compliance Solutions)
claim_supported: PRC-028-1 requires passive Sequence-of-Event Recording, Fault Recording, and continuous Dynamic Disturbance Recording equipment, naming commercial compliance hardware already on the market (SEL Intelligent Electronic Devices, SEL-3350/3555 Real Time Automation Controller, SEL-2407/2488 satellite-synchronized clocks) -- not active MW-scale field disturbance-injection test equipment. Compliance dates: BES IBRs in service by 4/1/2025 need 50% compliance by 4/1/2028, 100% by 1/1/2030; non-BES IBRs in service by 5/2026 need 100% by 1/1/2030.
locator: Article body: SER/FR/DDR definitions, named SEL equipment models, compliance-date table.
notes: Names Schweitzer Engineering Laboratories (SEL) as the actual incumbent equipment vendor being bought for this compliance regime -- a concrete competitor/substitute finding not in the idea's competition_outlook.

title: Navigating NERC PRC-029-1: A Field Guide for Inverter-Based Resource Owners
authors_or_org: Elevate Energy Consulting (founder Dr. Ryan Quint, former NERC Director of Engineering and Security Integration)
claim_supported: PRC-029-1 verification runs through two pathways: Design Compliance (EMT-model ride-through simulation) and Operational Compliance ('IBR plants ride through actual grid events, leveraging the disturbance monitoring equipment and measurements from NERC PRC-028-1') -- pre-commissioning simulation followed by real-world event capture via installed passive monitoring hardware, not mandatory field-injection testing during commissioning.
locator: Article body: 'Design Compliance' and 'Operational Compliance' section headers and definitions.
rejection_reason: P4 context citation; claim co-supported by primary sources; retained as working evidence, not a canonical atlas member
notes: Independent corroboration of S01/S02's finding from a former NERC engineering director's consultancy. Demoted: the 'model-based compliance is sufficient' conclusion is already established by T1 primary source P3R2-A-03-S01 (Texas RE) and independently corroborated by P3R2-A-03-S02 (which additionally names specific compliance hardware); this record restates the same conclusion without adding a new fact.

india_origin_audit.notes: Founder Dr. Ryan Quint based in Virginia, USA; prior NERC (Atlanta, GA) director.

title: TITAN Series High-Power Regenerative Grid Simulator (10 MVA)
claim_supported: ActionPower (SSE STAR Market 688719.SH, Xi'an, China) already sells a commercial, paralleled-to-10MVA grid simulator (TITAN Series) performing LVRT/HVRT/ZVRT fault-ride-through and grid-forming-converter validation with 1ms sub-cycle voltage interruptions -- but it is stationary laboratory/factory test equipment (fixed cabinets up to 7,720kg each), not field-trailerized.
locator: Product page: power range, ride-through test capability, cabinet dimensions/weight specs.
notes: Shows commercial MW-class grid-simulation hardware already exists (adding to NREL/Sandia PHIL and OPAL-RT/Typhoon already cited in the idea's competition_outlook), narrowing -- though not eliminating -- the claimed 'no MW field hardware exists' gap to specifically the trailerized/field-deployable form factor.

india_origin_audit.notes: HQ Xi'an, Shaanxi, China; SSE STAR Market listed. Not India.

title: DOE Approves Next Phase of Funding for Electron-Ion Collider (CD-3B, $67M long-lead procurements)
claim_supported: DOE approved Critical Decision 3B (Feb 6 2026) authorizing ~$67M of additional long-lead procurements for the Electron-Ion Collider (following ~$24M US + $8.3M overseas awarded under CD-3A, April 2024), moving the ~$1.6-2.6B project toward formal construction; DOE and EIC officials state the project targets operations '2030' per corroborating BNL/CERN Courier reporting.
locator: Article body: CD-3B funding figure, CD-3A prior-phase figures, named procurement items, named officials/quotes.
notes: Primary/official 2028-2035-window demand+timing trigger for US big-science vacuum/beam-pipe procurement, independent of the ITER tenders already in the atlas (L07-041/042/043).

india_origin_audit.notes: US DOE national laboratory, official .gov-affiliated domain.

title: Overview -- Proton Improvement Plan-II (PIP-II)
claim_supported: PIP-II is a new superconducting 800 MeV H- linac and beam-transfer line upgrading the Fermilab accelerator complex toward >1.2 MW proton power for LBNF/DUNE; project scheduled for completion in 2028, requiring new UHV beamline/cryomodule vacuum hardware.
locator: Project overview page: linac specification, LBNF/DUNE purpose, 2028 completion target.
notes: Independent second US national-lab accelerator-construction demand/timing trigger alongside the EIC (S01), both requiring NEG-class vacuum surface treatment on new beamline sections in the 2027-2030 window.

india_origin_audit.notes: US DOE national laboratory, official domain.

title: Build America, Buy America
authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy, Office of Management
claim_supported: The Build America, Buy America Act requires federally funded 'infrastructure projects' to use domestic iron, steel, manufactured products, and construction materials (>=55% US-content cost threshold for manufactured products), with DOE publishing agency-level waivers where domestic supply is unavailable.
locator: DOE BABA program landing page, directly WebFetched 2026-07-13: confirms 'an Agency may not obligate funds for an infrastructure project unless all of the iron, steel, manufactured products, and construction materials used in the project are produced in the United States or the Agency applies a waiver'; domestic-content threshold 'greater than 55 percent of the total cost of all components'; waiver requests subject to DOE/OMB review plus a public comment period of no less than 15 calendar days.
notes: CAVEAT: BABA targets federally funded 'infrastructure projects' (roads, water systems, broadband, etc.); its applicability to DOE national-lab R&D-facility component procurement (vs. construction materials) is not confirmed here and should not be treated as a guarantee that NEG-coated vacuum components specifically qualify for a domestic-sourcing mandate -- used only as directional policy context for the 'domestic-sourcing preference' claim in the idea's launch_2030_timing_thesis, not as a hard procurement rule for this product category.

title: Additions and Modifications to the Entity List; Removals From the Validated End-User (VEU) Program
authors_or_org: Bureau of Industry and Security, U.S. Department of Commerce
claim_supported: BIS final rule (89 FR 96830, published Dec 5 2024, effective Dec 2 2024) added 140 entities across China/Japan/South Korea/Singapore to the Entity List for involvement in advanced-node IC development/production or China's Military-Civil Fusion strategy, and removed three entities (CSMC, HHGrace, AMEC) from the Validated End-User program.
locator: Federal Register API record (federalregister.gov/api/v1/documents/2024-28267.json): title, abstract, publication/effective dates. federalregister.gov's own HTML page redirected to an anti-bot 'unblock' interstitial and could not be read directly this session (same access pattern noted in atlas record L08-043); the API JSON endpoint was used instead, consistent with prior-session practice.
notes: CRITICAL for the idea's 2027 export-control-counsel gate: the rule's own abstract does not name AMEC/NAURA/Piotech individually (140 entities are not itemized in the API abstract); the specific identification of NAURA and Piotech as newly Entity-Listed and AMEC as VEU-removed comes from corroborating secondary legal/trade analysis (S02, S03), which should be independently cross-checked against the full rule text (89 FR 96830, Entity List Supp. No. 4 to Part 744) by counsel before this is treated as dispositive.

institutions.name: Bureau of Industry and Security, U.S. Department of Commerce

india_origin_audit.notes: US federal agency; official Federal Register API.

title: US Department of Commerce Significantly Expands Controls Targeting Indigenous Production of Advanced Semiconductors in China
authors_or_org: Baker McKenzie (Global Sanctions and Export Controls Blog)
claim_supported: Directly WebFetched 2026-07-13: this Baker McKenzie post confirms the Dec 2024 BIS rule designated '140 new entities' including China-based indigenous tool manufacturers, semiconductor fabs, and investment companies to the Entity List, and separately removed three entities (CSMC, HHGrace, and AMEC) from the VEU program. The post does NOT itself name NAURA or Piotech among the 140 entities -- that more specific claim is not independently confirmed by this source alone (two direct re-fetch attempts targeting company-name mentions found no NAURA/Piotech text in the article).
locator: Direct WebFetch of sanctionsnews.bakermckenzie.com article body, 2026-07-13: '140 new entities' paragraph and 'Three entities (CSMC, HHGrace, and AMEC) were removed as eligible end-users from the VEU program' sentence.
rejection_reason: P4 context citation; claim co-supported by primary sources; retained as working evidence, not a canonical atlas member
notes: Demoted: the same evidence file (P3R2-A-10.md) cites T1 primary source P3R2-A-10-S01 (Federal Register, 89 FR 96830) for the same Entity List/VEU-removal event; this trade-press account does not independently confirm the specific NAURA/Piotech naming claim it was originally cited for, and duplicates the T1 source's core finding. Retained as working evidence only.

india_origin_audit.notes: Baker McKenzie is a global law firm headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, USA (Global Sanctions and Export Controls Blog is a firm publication).

title: U.S. Export Waiver Revocations Put Samsung and SK hynix in Limbo
claim_supported: BIS revoked Validated End-User authorizations for foreign-owned fabs in China (TSMC, Samsung, SK hynix) in a September 2025 rule, continuing a multi-year tightening trend in US export-control treatment of China-based semiconductor fabrication (Federal Register 2025-16735, published Sept 2 2025).
locator: Search-result synthesis of the EE Times article and the parallel Federal Register / CNBC / Tom's Hardware coverage of the same Sept 2025 VEU-revocation rule; NOT independently re-confirmed by direct WebFetch this session.
rejection_reason: inaccessible; cannot verify -- logged as rejected per claim discipline
notes: Three independent WebFetch attempts on eetimes.com this session (2026-07-13) all timed out (60s limit); page could not be fetched to confirm the claim. Context showing the export-control environment for China-facing semiconductor-equipment business is tightening further (not stabilizing) between the Dec 2024 rule (S01/S02) and mid-2026, relevant to assessing whether the idea's 2027 export-control-counsel gate will find a more permissive or a more restrictive environment.

india_origin_audit.notes: EE Times is a US-based (AspenCore) semiconductor trade publication.

title: Semion RFEA -- Ion Energy and Ion Flux Analysis System
claim_supported: Impedans (Dublin, Ireland, est. 2005) sells the Semion retarding-field-energy-analyzer product line -- a 3-/4-grid RFEA multi-sensor system (up to 13 sensing elements) measuring real-time ion energy distribution and ion flux, the closest existing commercial vendor-neutral IEDF metrology product to the idea's proposed sensor. Directly WebFetched 2026-07-13, confirmed: 'measures the ion flux and ion energy distribution hitting a surface in real-time,' 'Semion Multi Sensor system can have up to 13 sensing elements,' described as 'the industry standard for substrate level measurement of the ion energy distribution.'
locator: Product page: RFEA multi-grid sensor description, multi-sensor configuration (up to 13 elements); directly re-confirmed by WebFetch 2026-07-13.
notes: Named European incumbent in the IEDF-metrology sub-market; the idea's competition_outlook did not name a dedicated metrology-only competitor (only generator vendors AE/MKS/Comet), which is a gap this record fills.

title: CMA10B Mass Flow Controller
claim_supported: MKS already sells the CMA10B (C-Series) MFC with 'ultrafast response time of <100 msec' (<=100ms typical for 50sccm-5slm models) and pressure coefficient <0.025% of reading/psi -- i.e., a commercially shipping, pressure-insensitive, sub-100ms MFC -- but calibrated only for six non-reactive gases (He, Ar, air, N2, O2, SF6) and marketed for mass spectroscopy/vacuum coating/bioreactor applications, not named for semiconductor ALD/ALE reactive-precursor service.
locator: Product spec page: response-time, pressure-coefficient, and calibrated-gas-list fields.
notes: Important competitive-risk finding: the underlying sub-100ms pressure-insensitive MFC technology already exists commercially at MKS outside the semiconductor product line, meaning the idea's technical moat rests specifically on qualifying this performance class for corrosive/reactive semiconductor-precursor gas service with zero overshoot and inline transient verification -- a narrower, though still real, gap than 'no one has built sub-100ms MFCs.'

india_origin_audit.notes: MKS Instruments HQ Andover, Massachusetts, USA.

title: CRITERION Series (D700MG) High Speed & Precision Pressure Insensitive Mass Flow Module
claim_supported: CORRECTED after direct WebFetch 2026-07-13: this specific D700MG product page states '100ms Step-up Response & MFC-to-MFC deviation control' as a headline feature -- it does NOT state a ~1 second settling time, and does not describe a variable-PID/piezoelectric mechanism on this page. The original claim (drawn from a separate HORIBA SEC-Z700X/Z500X product page, not this URL) that HORIBA's line settles in ~1 second is NOT supported by this specific fetched page; on the contrary, this page's own advertised 100ms step-up response figure is in the same order of magnitude as the idea's <100ms target, which conflicts with the 'idea's target is a genuine ~10x gap' framing this record was originally cited to support. Flagging this discrepancy rather than silently repeating the unverified claim.
locator: Direct WebFetch of horiba.com D700MG product page, 2026-07-13: 'Features' section, '100ms Step-up Response & MFC-to-MFC deviation control' bullet. No other response-time, settling-time, PID, or piezoelectric language found on this specific page.
notes: Satisfies the Japan local-competitor requirement (HORIBA HQ Kyoto, Japan). IMPORTANT CORRECTION: the D700MG page itself (this URL) advertises 100ms step-up response, not the ~1-second settling figure originally attributed to it -- the idea's 'HORIBA is ~10x slower' competitive-differentiation claim should be re-verified against HORIBA's SEC-Z700X/Z500X pages specifically (a different product line) before being relied on for scoring; this record no longer independently supports that gap claim for the D700MG model.

title: Brooks Instrument Launches Ultra-Compact, High-Performance Mass Flow Controller (AMF Series)
claim_supported: CORRECTED after direct WebFetch 2026-07-13: Brooks Instrument's AMF Series launch (June 18, 2026) is confirmed for its footprint claim ('smallest footprint of any mass flow controller in its performance class,' about the size of a Post-it note pad), its 1000:1 turndown capability, and integrated EtherNet/IP digital communications/diagnostics. The specific '+/-0.6% accuracy' figure is NOT stated on the fetched page (the page only describes the product generically as 'highly accurate' without a numeric spec) -- that figure is not independently confirmed and should not be treated as sourced to this record.
locator: Direct WebFetch of brooksinstrument.com press release, 2026-07-13: launch-date/footprint sentence, 1000:1 turndown and EtherNet/IP feature bullets. No numeric accuracy percentage found in the fetched text.
notes: Shows incumbents are actively refreshing MFC product lines in 2026 (timing-window risk for a new entrant), even though this specific refresh does not directly target the transient-response niche.

india_origin_audit.notes: Brooks Instrument HQ Hatfield, Pennsylvania, USA.

title: Gas Delivery Systems (Talon Modular System)
claim_supported: CORRECTED after direct WebFetch 2026-07-13: Ichor Systems' own page describes its 'TMS modular gas delivery system' as supporting multiple connection types (Face Seal, C-Seal, W-Seal) while reducing footprint versus conventional gas panels -- confirming Ichor is a real, identifiable 'gas-panel integrator' company (the buyer archetype named in the idea). The page does NOT itself itemize integration of MFCs, valves, manifolds, sensors, and control electronics as a bundle -- that fuller BOM description is not independently confirmed by this specific fetched text. Lam Research + Applied Materials representing 76% of Ichor's FY2025 sales remains sourced to atlas L07-049 (Ichor FY2025 10-K), not to this page.
locator: Direct WebFetch of ichorsystems.com/gas-delivery-systems, 2026-07-13: TMS modular gas delivery system description (seal-configuration and footprint-reduction language). Integration-scope detail beyond this was not found on the fetched page.
notes: Confirms Ichor as the concrete gas-panel-integrator counterparty the idea's 2028 dual-sourcing LOI gate would need to clear; cross-reference atlas L07-049 (Ichor FY2025 10-K, Lam+AMAT=76% of sales) for the customer-concentration context that makes new-supplier qualification structurally hard (few, powerful buyers).

india_origin_audit.notes: Ichor Systems HQ Fremont, California, USA (Ichor Holdings, Ltd. parent).
